title: Ensure that the --tls-cert-file and --tls-private-key-file arguments are set as appropriate

input_row.title: Ensure that the --tls-cert-file and --tls-private-key-file arguments are set as appropriate
input_row.description: Setup TLS connection on the API server.
input_row.rationale: API server communication contains sensitive parameters that should remain encrypted in transit. Configure the API server to serve only HTTPS traffic. Impact: TLS and client certificate authentication must be configured for your Kubernetes cluster deployment.
input_row.audit: Run the following command on the Control Plane node: ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver Verify that the --tls-cert-file and --tls-private-key-file arguments exist and they are set as appropriate.
input_row.remediation: Follow the Kubernetes documentation and set up the TLS connection on the apiserver. Then, edit the API server pod specification file /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube- apiserver.yaml on the master node and set the TLS certificate and private key file parameters. --tls-cert-file=<path/to/tls-certificate-file> --tls-private-key-file=<path/to/tls-key-file> Default Value: By default, --tls-cert-file and --tls-private-key-file are presented and created for use. References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/command-line-tools-reference/kube- apiserver/ 2. https://github.com/kelseyhightower/docker-kubernetes-tls-guide
input_row.impact: TLS and client certificate authentication must be configured for your Kubernetes cluster deployment.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_api_server_tls_cert_and_key_file_configured

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Query the kube-apiserver Pod specification, typically located in the static Pod manifest at `/etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml`, using the Kubernetes API or by running `kubectl -n kube-system get pod -l component=kube-apiserver -o yaml` if the API server is managed as a Pod.
- Parse the `command` or `args` section of the kube-apiserver container to verify the presence of both `--tls-cert-file` and `--tls-private-key-file` flags.
- Extract the file paths provided to these flags and ensure they are non-empty and point to valid files (if file existence can be checked via a mounted volume in the Pod spec).
- Pass Criteria:
  - Both `--tls-cert-file` and `--tls-private-key-file` arguments are present in the kube-apiserver command/args.
  - The values for these arguments are non-empty and reference file paths.
- Fail Criteria:
  - Either argument is missing, empty, or not set.
- Example kubectl command:
  ```
  kubectl -n kube-system get pod -l component=kube-apiserver -o jsonpath='{.items[*].spec.containers[*].command}'
  ```
  or, for static Pod manifest inspection (requires host access or DaemonSet with hostPath mount):
  ```
  kubectl get node <control-plane-node> -o json | jq '.status.addresses'
  ```
  (then use automation to retrieve and parse `/etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml` from each control plane node)
- The check is fully automatable as the required flags are part of the kube-apiserver configuration, which is accessible via the Kubernetes API or by inspecting the Pod spec. No subjective or in-guest OS review is required.

**SOURCE:** CIS KUBERNETES BENCHMARK V1.11.1 PDF
